ddls:
CREATE TABLE "properties" (
  "property_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each property */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label of the property */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Description of the property */,
  "subPropertyOf" TEXT /* Property of which this is a sub-property */,
  "equivalentProperty" TEXT /* Property that is equivalent to this one */,
  "subproperties" TEXT /* List of sub-properties */,
  "domainIncludes" TEXT /* List of types that this property applies to */,
  "rangeIncludes" TEXT /* List of types that this property can have as a value */,
  "inverseOf" TEXT /* Property that is the inverse of this one */,
  "supersedes" TEXT /* Property that this one supersedes */,
  "supersededBy" TEXT /* Property that supersedes this one */,
  "isPartOf" TEXT /* Property of which this is a part */,
  "deprecated" INTEGER /* Whether the property is deprecated */,
  "expected_type" TEXT /* Expected type of the property value */,
  "usage_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes on property usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("property_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_properties_inverseOf FOREIGN KEY ("inverseOf") REFERENCES inverse_properties ("inverse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_properties_subPropertyOf FOREIGN KEY ("subPropertyOf") REFERENCES properties ("property_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_properties_domainIncludes FOREIGN KEY ("domainIncludes") REFERENCES domains ("domain_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_properties_equivalentProperty FOREIGN KEY ("equivalentProperty") REFERENCES properties ("property_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_properties_rangeIncludes FOREIGN KEY ("rangeIncludes") REFERENCES ranges ("range_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content item */,
  "item_type" TEXT /* Type of the content item */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the content item */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the content item */,
  "abstract" TEXT /* Abstract of the content item */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label associated with the content item */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment associated with the content item */,
  "accelerationTime" TEXT /* Acceleration time if applicable */,
  "acceptedAnswer" TEXT /* Accepted answer if the item is a question */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher of the content item */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the content item */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Publication date of the content item */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL of the content item image */,
  "thumbnail_url" TEXT /* URL of the content item thumbnail */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "domains" (
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each domain */,
  "domain_name" TEXT /* Name of the domain */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the domain */,
  "domain_url" TEXT /* URL of the domain */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("domain_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ranges" (
  "range_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each range */,
  "range_name" TEXT /* Name of the range */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the range */,
  "range_url" TEXT /* URL of the range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("range_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inverse_properties" (
  "inverse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inverse property */,
  "inverse_name" TEXT /* Name of the inverse property */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the inverse property */,
  "inverse_url" TEXT /* URL of the inverse property */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inverse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "superseded_properties" (
  "superseded_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each superseded property */,
  "original_property" TEXT /* The original property that has been superseded */,
  "new_property" TEXT /* The new property that supersedes the original */,
  "supersedence_date" TEXT /* The date when the supersedence occurred */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the supersedence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("superseded_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_superseded_properties_new_property FOREIGN KEY ("new_property") REFERENCES properties ("property_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_superseded_properties_original_property FOREIGN KEY ("original_property") REFERENCES properties ("property_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "property_values" (
  "value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each property value */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the content item */,
  "property_id" TEXT /* Foreign key referencing the property */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the property */,
  "value_type" TEXT /* Type of the property value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Description of the permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role identifier for the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Password hash for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the content item */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the content item */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the content item */,
  "dislikes" INTEGER /* Number of dislikes for the content item */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments for the content item */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of shares for the content item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the content item */,
  "related_item_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the related content item */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of the relationship (e.g. 'partOf', 'acceptedAnswer') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)
